mission.title: Install and Configure KubeRay
mission.description: KubeRay is an open-source Kubernetes operator that simplifies the deployment and management of Ray applications on Kubernetes. It is particularly useful for users looking to run distributed machine learning workloads efficiently. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes cluster and the complexity of managing Ray applications.

steps.title: Add KubeRay Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeRay Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeRay using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kuberay https://ray-project.github.io/kuberay

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the official KubeRay Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeRay CRDs
steps.description:
Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) are required for KubeRay to function properly. This step installs the necessary CRDs.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/raycluster.ray.io_rayclusters.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/rayjob.ray.io_rayjobs.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/rayservice.ray.io_rayservices.yaml

```

> Installs the RayCluster CRD.
> Installs the RayJob CRD.
> Installs the RayService CRD.

steps.title: Install KubeRay Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the KubeRay operator using Helm, which manages the lifecycle of Ray applications on Kubernetes.

```bash

helm install kuberay kuberay/kuberay --version 1.5.1 --namespace kuberay --create-namespace

```

> Installs the KubeRay operator in the 'kuberay' namespace.

steps.title: Verify KubeRay installation
steps.description:
After installation, it's important to verify that the KubeRay operator is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

kubectl get crd

```

> Check that the KubeRay operator pod is running.
> Verify that the CRDs are installed.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeRay
uninstall.description:
This step removes the KubeRay operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all RayClusters, RayJobs, and RayServices, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kuberay --namespace kuberay

kubectl delete namespace kuberay

```

> Uninstalls the KubeRay operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeRay
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step involves upgrading the KubeRay operator to a newer version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kuberay -o wide

helm upgrade kuberay kuberay/kuberay --version 1.5.1 --namespace kuberay

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

```

> Check the status of the current pods before upgrading.
> Upgrades the KubeRay operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the KubeRay operator pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error from server (NotFound): customresourcedefinitions.apiextensions.k8s.io "rayclusters.ray.io" not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the RayCluster CRD was not installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Re-run the command to install the RayCluster CRD: kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/raycluster.ray.io_rayclusters.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to recognize "": no matches for kind "RayCluster" in version "ray.io/v1alpha1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the KubeRay operator is not running or the CRDs are not registered.

**Fix:**
Ensure the KubeRay operator pod is running: kubectl get pods -n kuberay

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'Pending' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may be due to insufficient resources in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Check resource allocation and ensure there are enough nodes with the required resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Job failed: exit code 1'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the RayJob encountered an error during execution.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the RayJob for more details: kubectl logs <rayjob-pod-name> -n kuberay

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Ray features, improved CRD management, and enhanced logging capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KubeRay operator pod running with a status of 'Running' when you execute: kubectl get pods -n kuberay.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the required resources.
